title: Broken 'start of day' modifier on date/time functions
description:
drh added on 2017-03-02 23:20:45:
Beginning with check-in [768c9859b21a3c8] the SQL
SELECT datetime(2457754, 'start of day');
is return NULL instead of '2016-12-31 00:00:00'.  Issue reported on the
SQLite mailing list by Mark Brand.
key_bug_features: Broken 'start of day' modifier in date/time functions
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_date INTEGER, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO test_dates (event_date, description) VALUES (2457754, 'Test event with start of day');
SELECT datetime(event_date, 'start of day') FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1;
SELECT datetime(event_date, 'start of day') AS start_of_day, description FROM test_dates;
SELECT datetime(NULL, 'start of day') AS null_case;
